```{r bh_dei_indices_triple, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## URM-axis triple (for the BOTTOM panel of each Index minipage).
fit_triple_axis <- function(d, idx, proxy_b) {
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "I(-sc_overall_z) ~ %s + %s + I(%s * %s) + i(urm_axis_blind, ref = 'Blind') + i(urm_axis_blind, %s, ref = 'Blind') + i(urm_axis_blind, %s, ref = 'Blind') + i(urm_axis_blind, I(%s * %s), ref = 'Blind') + resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3) | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income",
      idx, proxy_b, idx, proxy_b, idx, proxy_b, idx, proxy_b)),
    data = d, vcov = ~ responseid
  )))
}

groups_rg <- c("White_Male"     = "White Male",
               "White_Female"   = "White Female",
               "Asian_Male"     = "Asian Male",
               "Asian_Female"   = "Asian Female",
               "Hispanic_Male"  = "Hispanic Male",
               "Hispanic_Female"= "Hispanic Female")

build_top_tbl <- function(idx, lbl) {
  m_hr  <- fit_triple_rg(dt_hr,  idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  m_eng <- fit_triple_rg(dt_eng, idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  models_trp <- list("HR" = m_hr, "Eng" = m_eng)

  ## Dict: only the 6 triple terms get readable labels (one per demographic).
  dict_top <- setNames(
    paste0(unname(groups_rg), " $\\times$ DEI Index $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn"),
    paste0("race_gender::", names(groups_rg), ":I(", idx, " * firm_dei_attention_z)")
  )

  tbl <- etable(
    models_trp, tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
    placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
    keep = "%race_gender::.*:I\\(",
    fitstat = NA,
    drop.section = "fixef",
    digits = "r3", digits.stats = 3, se.below = TRUE,
    signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
    dict = dict_top,
    notes = NULL
  )
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\toprule",
    paste0(
      "\\\\multicolumn{3}{c}{\\\\textbf{", lbl, " --- Per Race-Gender (triple terms)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
      "\\\\toprule",
      " & \\\\textbf{HR} & \\\\textbf{Eng} \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
    ),
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\raggedright \\\\scriptsize", tbl)
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
    "\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.72}\\\\begin{tabular}",
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}\\[H\\]\\s*\\n\\\\centering\\s*\\n", "", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{table\\}\\s*$", "", tbl)
  tbl
}

build_bot_tbl <- function(idx, lbl) {
  m_hr  <- fit_triple_urm(dt_hr,  idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  m_eng <- fit_triple_urm(dt_eng, idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  models_trp <- list("HR" = m_hr, "Eng" = m_eng)

  dict_trp <- c(
    "urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_URM"                         = "URM",
    "urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_NonURM"                      = "Non-URM",
    setNames("URM $\\times$ DEI Index",     paste0("urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_URM:", idx)),
    setNames("Non-URM $\\times$ DEI Index", paste0("urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_NonURM:", idx)),
    "urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_URM:firm_dei_attention_z"    = "URM $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
    "urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_NonURM:firm_dei_attention_z" = "Non-URM $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
    setNames("URM $\\times$ DEI Index $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
             paste0("urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_URM:I(", idx, " * firm_dei_attention_z)")),
    setNames("Non-URM $\\times$ DEI Index $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn",
             paste0("urm_tech_blind::Nonblind_NonURM:I(", idx, " * firm_dei_attention_z)"))
  )

  tbl <- etable(
    models_trp, tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
    placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
    keep = "%urm_tech_blind",
    fitstat = "n",
    drop.section = "fixef",
    digits = "r3", digits.stats = 3, se.below = TRUE,
    signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
    dict = dict_trp,
    notes = NULL,
    extralines = NULL
  )
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\toprule",
    paste0(
      "\\\\multicolumn{3}{c}{\\\\textbf{", lbl, " --- URM Cut (full triple)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
      "\\\\toprule",
      " & \\\\textbf{HR} & \\\\textbf{Eng} \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
    ),
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\raggedright \\\\scriptsize", tbl)
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
    "\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.72}\\\\begin{tabular}",
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("Resume \\(Content\\) FE",
             "\\\\midrule\nResume (Content) FE", tbl, perl = TRUE)
  tbl <- sub("Observations",
             "\\\\midrule\nObservations", tbl, perl = TRUE)
  tbl <- gsub("(?:\\\\midrule\\s*){2,}", "\\\\midrule\n", tbl, perl = TRUE)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}\\[H\\]\\s*\\n\\\\centering\\s*\\n", "", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{table\\}\\s*$", "", tbl)
  tbl
}

## Axis-cut triple, full 12 coefficients (levels, x DEI, x Firm DEI Attn, triple).
## Mirrors build_bot_tbl in coverage; drops FE / fitstat (in middle block).
build_axis_tbl <- function(idx, lbl) {
  m_hr  <- fit_triple_axis(dt_hr,  idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  m_eng <- fit_triple_axis(dt_eng, idx, "firm_dei_attention_z")
  models_trp <- list("HR" = m_hr, "Eng" = m_eng)

  cells <- c("Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic")
  labs  <- c("Male (non-hispanic)", "Female (non-hispanic)", "Hispanic")

  dict_axis <- c(
    setNames(labs, paste0("urm_axis_blind::", cells)),
    setNames(paste0(labs, " $\\times$ DEI Index"),
             paste0("urm_axis_blind::", cells, ":", idx)),
    setNames(paste0(labs, " $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn"),
             paste0("urm_axis_blind::", cells, ":firm_dei_attention_z")),
    setNames(paste0(labs, " $\\times$ DEI $\\times$ Firm DEI Attn"),
             paste0("urm_axis_blind::", cells, ":I(", idx, " * firm_dei_attention_z)"))
  )

  tbl <- etable(
    models_trp, tex = TRUE, style.tex = style.tex("aer"),
    placement = "H", depvar = FALSE,
    keep = "%urm_axis_blind",
    fitstat = NA,
    drop.section = "fixef",
    digits = "r3", digits.stats = 3, se.below = TRUE,
    signif.code = c("***" = 0.01, "**" = 0.05, "*" = 0.1),
    dict = dict_axis,
    notes = NULL
  )
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\toprule",
    paste0(
      "\\\\multicolumn{3}{c}{\\\\textbf{", lbl, " --- URM Axis (full triple)}} \\\\\\\\ ",
      "\\\\toprule",
      " & \\\\textbf{HR} & \\\\textbf{Eng} \\\\\\\\ \\\\addlinespace[2pt]"
    ),
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- gsub("\\\\par \\\\raggedright", "\\\\par \\\\raggedright \\\\scriptsize", tbl)
  tbl <- sub(
    "\\\\begin\\{tabular\\}",
    "\\\\resizebox{\\\\textwidth}{!}{\\\\renewcommand{\\\\arraystretch}{0.72}\\\\begin{tabular}",
    tbl
  )
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{tabular\\}", "\\\\end{tabular}}", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\begin\\{table\\}\\[H\\]\\s*\\n\\\\centering\\s*\\n", "", tbl)
  tbl <- sub("\\\\end\\{table\\}\\s*$", "", tbl)
  tbl
}

## Each minipage stacks: top (per-RG triple terms only) and axis (URM-axis full
## triple, 12 coefficients). The URM-cut block is dropped because the axis block
## subsumes it: URM = NHF + Hispanic, Non-URM = NHM (Non-Hisp Male).
top_A  <- build_top_tbl (indices[1], idx_labels[1]); axis_A <- build_axis_tbl(indices[1], idx_labels[1])
top_B  <- build_top_tbl (indices[2], idx_labels[2]); axis_B <- build_axis_tbl(indices[2], idx_labels[2])

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\\centering\n")
cat("\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.32\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat(top_A); cat("\\par\\vspace{0.3em}\n"); cat(axis_A)
cat("\\end{minipage}\\hfill\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[t]{0.32\\textwidth}\\centering\n")
cat(top_B); cat("\\par\\vspace{0.3em}\n"); cat(axis_B)
cat("\\end{minipage}\\hfill\n")
cat("\\par\\vspace{0.4em}\n")
cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}{\\textwidth}\\raggedright\\scriptsize\n")
cat("Top panel: per-race-gender triple (only the 6 triple terms shown). Bottom panel: URM-axis triple (full 12 coefficients), where Male (non-hispanic) = WM+AM, Female (non-hispanic) = WF+AF, Hispanic = HM+HF. The URM-cut block is omitted: it is implied by the axis block (URM = Female (non-hispanic) + Hispanic; Non-URM = Male (non-hispanic)). Both blocks share identical specification: Resume (Content) FE $\\checkmark$, Display Position FE $\\checkmark$, Respondent FE $\\times$, Respondent Characteristics $\\checkmark$.\\\\\n")
cat("Standard errors clustered by respondent.\\\\\n")
cat("\\textit{Note:} Firm DEI Attn proxy is \\texttt{firm\\_dei\\_attention\\_z} (within-cell z-score of the equal-weight average of \\texttt{firm\\_attention\\_race\\_z} and \\texttt{firm\\_attention\\_gender\\_z}).\\\\\n")
cat("Positive coefficient $\\Rightarrow$ effect of blinding raises the score for that group.\n")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n")

rm(dt_hr, dt_eng, indices, idx_labels, chk, nox, groups_rg,
   shared_notes, extralines_4, build_axis,
   fit_triple_rg, fit_triple_urm, fit_triple_axis,
   build_top_tbl, build_bot_tbl, build_axis_tbl,
   top_A, top_B, axis_A, axis_B)
```


\newpage
# Treatment Effect on Productivity of Hired (The Efficiency Myth)

\vspace{-0.4em}
If blind hiring reshuffles outcomes (Sections 2--4), proponents might argue it is a necessary trade-off for strict meritocracy and better technical hires. Our results show otherwise.
\vspace{-0.4em}

\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
  \item \textbf{Signal Extraction Remains Flat (S5.1.):} Removing demographic information (or noise) does \emph{not} meaningfully improve their ability to identify top ability candidates.
  \item \textbf{Quality of Hires Does Not Improve (S5.2.):} Blind hiring has a \emph{null} effect on the actual coding quality of the hired cohort.
\end{itemize}
\vspace{-1em}

## Does Blinding Lead to More Accurate Coding Evaluations?

\textcolor{blue}{\textbf{Takeaway}}: Evaluators do extract signal about candidates' actual coding ability --- Engineers much more strongly than HR, consistent with Engineers seeing the code while HR sees only the resume. Blinding, however, does \emph{not} meaningfully change this signal-extraction ability in either role: the $\texttt{true\_coding\_z} \times \texttt{blind}$ interaction is small, negative, and statistically insignificant for both HR and Engineers. If blinding --- which in our experiment amounts to removing random, uninformative demographic noise --- improved signal-extraction ability, we would expect a positive and statistically significant interaction term. In short, blinding \emph{reshuffles} who receives high scores (Section 1.1) but does not change \emph{how well} those scores track actual coding skill.

\textcolor{blue}{\textbf{Specification}}:
\vspace{-0.7em}
$$
\text{Hiring Evaluation Score (Z)} \sim \text{True Coding Ability (Z)} \times \mathbbm{1}[\text{Blind arm}] \mid \text{Respondent FE} + \text{Display Position FE}.
$$
\vspace{-1.4em}
\begin{itemize}\setlength\itemsep{0pt}\setlength\parskip{0pt}\setlength\topsep{0pt}\setlength\partopsep{0pt}
    \item $H_0\!: \beta = 0$ --- the evaluator extracts \emph{no signal} from true coding ability.
    \item $H_0\!: \beta = 1$ --- the evaluator is \emph{perfectly calibrated}: a $1$ SD rise in true ability produces exactly a $1$ SD rise in the rating, with no shrinkage.
\end{itemize}

```{r blind_hiring_signal_extraction, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
## Pooled z-score of the lab coding score (constant within resume_index)
firm_long_dt[, true_coding_z := (overall_score - mean(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)) /
                                  sd(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)]

## Two pooled interacted regressions --- one per role. Since treat is
## between-respondent, responseid FE absorbs the main effect of treat but
## leaves both the main slope on true_coding_z (identified from within-
## respondent variation, by definition the nonblind arm's slope since
## nonblind is the reference) and the interaction slope identifiable.
m_hr  <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_coding_z ~ true_coding_z * i(treat, ref = "nonblind")
        | responseid + r_do,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
))
m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  feols(sc_coding_z ~ true_coding_z * i(treat, ref = "nonblind")
        | responseid + r_do,
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
))

## Extract arm-specific slopes, SEs, and the four tests (H0: beta = 0 / 1
## for each arm) from a single pooled model. beta_nonblind = main coef;
## beta_blind = main + interaction; SE(beta_blind) via linear-combination
## formula using the full vcov matrix.
extract_stats <- function(m) {
  ct    <- coeftable(m)
  V     <- vcov(m)
  main_name <- "true_coding_z"
  int_name  <- "true_coding_z:treat::blind"

  b_main <- ct[main_name, "Estimate"]
  s_main <- ct[main_name, "Std. Error"]
  b_int  <- ct[int_name,  "Estimate"]
  s_int  <- ct[int_name,  "Std. Error"]
  cov_mi <- V[main_name, int_name]

  b_nb <- b_main
  s_nb <- s_main
  b_b  <- b_main + b_int
  s_b  <- sqrt(s_main^2 + s_int^2 + 2 * cov_mi)

  pval <- function(t) 2 * (1 - pnorm(abs(t)))
  p_nb_0 <- pval(b_nb / s_nb)
  p_nb_1 <- pval((b_nb - 1) / s_nb)
  p_b_0  <- pval(b_b  / s_b)
  p_b_1  <- pval((b_b  - 1) / s_b)
  ## H0: beta_blind = beta_nonblind is the same as H0: beta_interaction = 0,
  ## so we read its p-value directly off the interaction coefficient.
  p_eq   <- ct[int_name, 4]

  fs <- fitstat(m, c("r2", "ar2"), simplify = TRUE)
  list(b_nb = unname(b_nb), s_nb = unname(s_nb),
       b_b  = unname(b_b),  s_b  = unname(s_b),
       p_nb_0 = p_nb_0, p_nb_1 = p_nb_1,
       p_b_0  = p_b_0,  p_b_1  = p_b_1,
       p_eq   = unname(p_eq),
       n = m$nobs, r2 = unname(fs["r2"]), ar2 = unname(fs["ar2"]))
}

s_hr  <- extract_stats(m_hr)
s_eng <- extract_stats(m_eng)

## Formatting helpers.
sig_stars <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p))        ""
  else if (p < 0.01)   "***"
  else if (p < 0.05)   "**"
  else if (p < 0.10)   "*"
  else                 ""
}
fmt_p <- function(p) {
  if (is.na(p)) return("")
  pstr <- if (p < 0.001) "$<\\!0.001$" else sprintf("%.3f", p)
  paste0("p = ", pstr, sig_stars(p))
}

chk <- "\\checkmark"
nox <- "$\\times$"

cell_col <- function(s) c(
  sprintf("%.3f~(%.3f)", s$b_nb, s$s_nb),
  sprintf("%.3f~(%.3f)", s$b_b,  s$s_b),
  format(s$n,   big.mark = ","),
  sprintf("%.3f", s$r2),
  sprintf("%.3f", s$ar2),
  nox, chk, chk, nox
)

tbl <- data.frame(
  lbl = c("$\\hat\\beta_{\\text{nonblind}}$ (slope, nonblind arm)",
          "$\\hat\\beta_{\\text{blind}}$ (slope, blind arm)",
          "Observations",
          "$R^2$",
          "Adj. $R^2$",
          "Resume (Content) FE",
          "Display Position FE",
          "Respondent FE",
          "Respondent Characteristics"),
  HR  = cell_col(s_hr),
  Eng = cell_col(s_eng),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE,
  check.names = FALSE
)
names(tbl) <- c(" ", "HR", "Engineer")

## Build the plot and save it to disk so we can include it via
## \includegraphics inside a LaTeX minipage layout.
plot_data <- data.frame(
  role = factor(c("HR", "HR", "Engineer", "Engineer"),
                levels = c("HR", "Engineer")),
  arm  = factor(c("Nonblind", "Blind", "Nonblind", "Blind"),
                levels = c("Nonblind", "Blind")),
  est  = c(s_hr$b_nb,  s_hr$b_b,  s_eng$b_nb, s_eng$b_b),
  se   = c(s_hr$s_nb,  s_hr$s_b,  s_eng$s_nb, s_eng$s_b)
)
plot_data$ci_lo <- plot_data$est - 1.96 * plot_data$se
plot_data$ci_hi <- plot_data$est + 1.96 * plot_data$se

p_signal <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = est, y = arm, color = arm)) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = ci_lo, xmax = ci_hi), height = 0.2, linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  facet_wrap(~ role, ncol = 1, strip.position = "left") +
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(-0.05, 1.1), breaks = seq(0, 1, 0.2)) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Nonblind" = "#d62728", "Blind" = "#1f77b4")) +
  labs(x = expression(hat(beta)*": slope of evaluator rating on true coding ability"),
       y = NULL, color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        strip.text.y.left = element_text(angle = 0, face = "bold"))

## Save plot to disk so we can include it via \includegraphics inside a
## LaTeX minipage (table on left, plot on right).
fig_dir <- here("2_Reports", "figures")
if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
plot_file <- file.path(fig_dir, "8B_signal_extraction.pdf")
suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
  ggsave(plot_file, plot = p_signal, width = 4.5, height = 2.6, device = "pdf")
))

cat("\\begin{table}[H]\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[c]{0.50\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("{\\fontsize{7}{9}\\selectfont\n")

## Plain kable + row_spec (no kable_styling, since kable_styling adds an
## inner \\begin{table} wrapper that LaTeX rejects inside a minipage).
print(
  kable(tbl, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE,
        align = "lcc", row.names = FALSE) %>%
    row_spec(2, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule") %>%
    row_spec(5, extra_latex_after = "\\midrule")
)

cat("}\n")
cat("\\end{minipage}%\n")
cat("\\begin{minipage}[c]{0.48\\textwidth}\n")
cat("\\centering\n")
cat("\\includegraphics[width=\\textwidth]{", plot_file, "}\n", sep = "")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")

cat("\\par\\vspace{0.4em}\n")
cat("\\noindent\\begin{minipage}{0.98\\textwidth}\\scriptsize\n")
cat("Cluster-robust standard errors (by respondent) shown in parentheses next to each $\\hat\\beta$. Stars on p-values: * $p<0.10$, ** $p<0.05$, *** $p<0.01$. \\textit{Note:} Both arm-specific slopes come from a single pooled interacted regression per role, \\texttt{sc\\_coding\\_z \\textasciitilde\\ true\\_coding\\_z * i(treat, ref = 'nonblind') | responseid + r\\_do}; $\\hat\\beta_{\\text{nonblind}}$ is read off the main coefficient, and $\\hat\\beta_{\\text{blind}}$ is computed as (main + interaction) with SE derived via the standard linear-combination formula using the estimated variance--covariance matrix. The ``Resume (Content) FE'' row is marked as not included because \\texttt{overall\\_score} is a resume-level property (one lab score per resume), so a resume-content FE would absorb the RHS and leave the main slope unidentified. ``Respondent Characteristics'' is marked as not included because respondent-level variables (age, gender, race, education, income) are all perfectly collinear with the respondent FE and therefore absorbed by it.\n")
cat("\\end{minipage}\n")
cat("\\end{table}\n\n")

rm(m_hr, m_eng, s_hr, s_eng,
   extract_stats, sig_stars, fmt_p, cell_col,
   chk, nox, tbl, plot_data, p_signal, plot_file, fig_dir)
```

## Does Blind Hiring Select Better Coders?


```{r blind_hiring_coding_quality, echo=FALSE, results='asis', warning=FALSE, message=FALSE, fig.width=10, fig.height=5}

# ==============================================================================
# HELPER: Dynamic standardization relative to nonblind group at each k
# ==============================================================================
get_avg_coding_score <- function(role_filter, treat_filter, k, 
                                  pass_var_prefix, data) {
  col <- paste0(pass_var_prefix, k)
  d   <- data[role == role_filter & treat == treat_filter & get(col) == 1]
  if (nrow(d) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  mean(d$overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)
}

# ==============================================================================
# COMPUTE TREATMENT EFFECT AT EACH K
# ==============================================================================
results_list <- list()

for (k in 1:18) {

  # --- HR ---
  hr_col  <- paste0("hr_pass", k)
  hr_data <- firm_long_dt[role == "HR" & get(hr_col) == 1]

  # Dynamic standardization: relative to nonblind mean/sd at this k
  ctrl_mean_hr <- hr_data[treat == "nonblind", mean(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)]
  ctrl_sd_hr   <- hr_data[treat == "nonblind", sd(overall_score,   na.rm = TRUE)]

  if (!is.na(ctrl_sd_hr) && ctrl_sd_hr > 0) {
    hr_data[, overall_score_std := (overall_score - ctrl_mean_hr) / ctrl_sd_hr]

    tryCatch({
      m_hr <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
        feols(overall_score_std ~ i(treat, ref = "nonblind") + resp_age |
                r_do + q_version + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income,
              data = hr_data, vcov = ~ responseid)
      ))
      ct <- coeftable(m_hr)
      if ("treat::blind" %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- ct["treat::blind", "Estimate"]
        se    <- ct["treat::blind", "Std. Error"]
        p_val <- ct["treat::blind", 4]
        results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
          Stage = "HR Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }, error = function(e) {})
  }

  # --- Engineer ---
  eng_col  <- paste0("eng_top", k)
  eng_data <- firm_long_dt[role == "Eng" & get(eng_col) == 1]

  ctrl_mean_eng <- eng_data[treat == "nonblind", mean(overall_score, na.rm = TRUE)]
  ctrl_sd_eng   <- eng_data[treat == "nonblind", sd(overall_score,   na.rm = TRUE)]

  if (!is.na(ctrl_sd_eng) && ctrl_sd_eng > 0) {
    eng_data[, overall_score_std := (overall_score - ctrl_mean_eng) / ctrl_sd_eng]

    tryCatch({
      m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
        feols(overall_score_std ~ i(treat, ref = "nonblind") + resp_age |
                r_do + q_version + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income +
                resp_python + resp_java,
              data = eng_data, vcov = ~ responseid)
      ))
      ct <- coeftable(m_eng)
      if ("treat::blind" %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- ct["treat::blind", "Estimate"]
        se    <- ct["treat::blind", "Std. Error"]
        p_val <- ct["treat::blind", 4]
        results_list[[length(results_list) + 1]] <- data.table(
          Stage = "Engineer Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }, error = function(e) {})
  }
}

dt_plot <- rbindlist(results_list)
dt_plot[, Stage := factor(Stage, levels = c("HR Stage", "Engineer Stage"))]
dt_plot[, sig_stars := fcase(
  p_val < 0.01, "***",
  p_val < 0.05, "**",
  p_val < 0.10, "*",
  default = ""
)]

# ==============================================================================
# PLOT
# ==============================================================================
ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = k, y = estimate, color = Stage)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
                  linewidth = 0.8, size = 0.6, fatten = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(y = ci_high, label = sig_stars),
            vjust = -0.5, size = 5, color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_wrap(~ Stage, ncol = 2) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("HR Stage" = "#C44E52", "Engineer Stage" = "#4C72B0")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:18, name = "Number of Candidates Selected (k)") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.15))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Does Blind Hiring Select Better Coders?",
    subtitle = paste("Effect of blinding on actual coding quality of selected candidates.",
                     "Standardized relative to nonblind group at each k."),
    y       = "Effect on Actual Coding Score (SD, relative to nonblind)",
    caption = "Positive = blind hiring selects candidates with higher actual coding scores.\nSignificance: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "none",
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 12),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.caption       = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0)
  )

rm(results_list, dt_plot, k,
   hr_col, hr_data, ctrl_mean_hr, ctrl_sd_hr, m_hr, ct,
   eng_col, eng_data, ctrl_mean_eng, ctrl_sd_eng, m_eng,
   est, se, p_val)
```

```{r blind_hiring_demographic_selection, eval = FALSE, echo=FALSE, fig.width=12, fig.height=10}

# ==============================================================================
# COMPUTE TREATMENT EFFECT AT EACH K
# LHS: hr_passk / eng_topk (0/1 selected)
# RHS: race_gender with "Blind" as reference, then flip signs
# ==============================================================================
results_all <- list()

demo_labels <- c("White_Male", "White_Female", "Asian_Male",
                 "Asian_Female", "Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female")

for (k in 1:18) {

  hr_col  <- paste0("hr_pass", k)
  eng_col <- paste0("eng_top", k)

  # --- HR ---
  tryCatch({
    m_hr <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
      feols(as.formula(paste0(hr_col,
        " ~ i(race_gender, ref = 'Blind') |
          r_do + q_version + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income")),
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "HR"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
    ))
    ct <- coeftable(m_hr)
    for (dg in demo_labels) {
      coef_nm <- paste0("race_gender::", dg)
      if (coef_nm %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- -ct[coef_nm, "Estimate"]      # flip sign
        se    <-  ct[coef_nm, "Std. Error"]
        p_val <-  ct[coef_nm, 4]
        results_all[[length(results_all) + 1]] <- data.table(
          demo  = gsub("_", " ", dg),
          Stage = "HR Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) {})

  # --- Engineer ---
  tryCatch({
    m_eng <- suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(
      feols(as.formula(paste0(eng_col,
        " ~ i(race_gender, ref = 'Blind') + resp_age |
          r_do + q_version + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income +
          resp_python + resp_java")),
        data = firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"],
        vcov = ~ responseid)
    ))
    ct <- coeftable(m_eng)
    for (dg in demo_labels) {
      coef_nm <- paste0("race_gender::", dg)
      if (coef_nm %in% rownames(ct)) {
        est   <- -ct[coef_nm, "Estimate"]      # flip sign
        se    <-  ct[coef_nm, "Std. Error"]
        p_val <-  ct[coef_nm, 4]
        results_all[[length(results_all) + 1]] <- data.table(
          demo  = gsub("_", " ", dg),
          Stage = "Engineer Stage", k = k,
          estimate = est,
          ci_low   = est - 1.96 * se,
          ci_high  = est + 1.96 * se,
          p_val    = p_val
        )
      }
    }
  }, error = function(e) {})
}

# ==============================================================================
# ASSEMBLE AND PLOT
# ==============================================================================
dt_plot <- rbindlist(results_all)
dt_plot[, Stage := factor(Stage, levels = c("HR Stage", "Engineer Stage"))]
dt_plot[, demo  := factor(demo,  levels = c("White Male",    "White Female",
                                             "Asian Male",    "Asian Female",
                                             "Hispanic Male", "Hispanic Female"))]
dt_plot[, sig_stars := fcase(
  p_val < 0.01, "***",
  p_val < 0.05, "**",
  p_val < 0.10, "*",
  default = ""
)]
dt_plot[, star_y := ifelse(estimate >= 0,
                            ci_high + 0.005,
                            ci_low  - 0.005)]

ggplot(dt_plot, aes(x = k, y = estimate, color = Stage)) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50", linewidth = 0.6) +
  geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = ci_low, ymax = ci_high),
                  linewidth = 0.6, size = 0.4, fatten = 3) +
  geom_text(aes(y = star_y, label = sig_stars),
            vjust = ifelse(dt_plot$estimate >= 0, 0, 1),
            size = 3, color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
  facet_grid(demo ~ Stage) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("HR Stage" = "#C44E52", "Engineer Stage" = "#4C72B0")) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(1, 18, by = 3),
                     name   = "Number of Candidates Selected (k)") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))) +
  labs(
    title    = "Effect of Blind Hiring on Selection Probability by Demographic Group",
    subtitle = paste("Blinding effect = -(effect of revealing identity vs blind).",
                     "Positive = more likely selected under blinding."),
    y        = "Blinding Effect on Selection Probability",
    color    = "Evaluator Role",
    caption  = "Significance: * p<0.10, ** p<0.05, *** p<0.01. Base group: Blind resumes."
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    legend.position    = "bottom",
    legend.title       = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.text        = element_text(size = 9),
    plot.title         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13),
    plot.subtitle      = element_text(size = 10, color = "gray30"),
    strip.text         = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    strip.text.y       = element_text(angle = 0),
    panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
    axis.title.x       = element_text(margin = margin(t = 10)),
    axis.title.y       = element_text(margin = margin(r = 10)),
    plot.caption       = element_text(size = 8, hjust = 0),
    panel.spacing      = unit(0.4, "lines")
  )

rm(results_all, dt_plot, demo_labels, dg, k,
   hr_col, eng_col, m_hr, m_eng, ct,
   est, se, p_val, coef_nm)
```



\clearpage
# Heterogeneity in Treatment Effects by Candidate Quality (Appendix)

\textit{The §2 demographic-effect regression run on subsamples by candidate quality. Three cuts: per-cell (6 race-gender groups), URM-tech (URM = WF+AF+HM+HF vs Non-URM = WM+AM), and URM-axis (Male non-Hispanic / Female non-Hispanic / Hispanic). Two splits per cut: GPA tier (Low / Mid / Top) and Coding tier (Bottom / Top half on \texttt{test\_case}). LHS sign-flipped so positive $\Rightarrow$ blinding raises the score.}
\vspace{-0.4em}


```{r appendix_het_by_quality_graphs, echo = FALSE, results = 'asis', warning = FALSE, message = FALSE, fig.width = 11, fig.height = 6}

dt_hr  <- firm_long_dt[role == "HR"]
dt_eng <- firm_long_dt[role == "Eng"]

build_aux <- function(d) {
  d[, urm_tech_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                    "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 1, "Nonblind_URM",
    treat == "nonblind" & urm_tech == 0, "Nonblind_NonURM",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_tech_blind := factor(urm_tech_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_URM", "Nonblind_NonURM"))]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := fcase(
    treat == "blind",                                                            "Blind",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Male", "Asian_Male"),         "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("White_Female", "Asian_Female"),     "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp",
    treat == "nonblind" & race_gender %in% c("Hispanic_Male", "Hispanic_Female"), "Nonblind_Hispanic",
    default = NA_character_)]
  d[, urm_axis_blind := factor(urm_axis_blind,
    levels = c("Blind", "Nonblind_MaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_FemaleNonHisp", "Nonblind_Hispanic"))]
  d
}
dt_hr  <- build_aux(dt_hr)
dt_eng <- build_aux(dt_eng)

## Helper: extract estimates from a feols split-fit, restricted to the
## demographic factor's coefficient rows. Returns a long data frame with
## Term / Tier / Estimate / SE / stars columns.
extract_split_te <- function(model_split, factor_var, tier_labels) {
  parts <- list()
  for (i in seq_along(model_split)) {
    df <- as.data.frame(coeftable(model_split[[i]]))
    df$Term <- rownames(df)
    df$Tier <- tier_labels[i]
    parts[[i]] <- df
  }
  out <- do.call(rbind, parts)
  out <- out[grepl(paste0("^", factor_var, "::"), out$Term), ]
  out$Term <- gsub(paste0(factor_var, "::"), "", out$Term)
  out$Term <- gsub("Nonblind_", "", out$Term)
  out$Term <- gsub("MaleNonHisp",   "Male (NH)",   out$Term)
  out$Term <- gsub("FemaleNonHisp", "Female (NH)", out$Term)
  out$Term <- gsub("_", " ", out$Term)
  out$Term <- factor(out$Term, levels = unique(out$Term))
  out$stars <- ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.01, "***",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.05, "**",
                ifelse(out$`Pr(>|t|)` < 0.10, "*", "")))
  out$Tier <- factor(out$Tier, levels = tier_labels)
  out
}

make_split_plot <- function(plot_df, title_label, color_vals, shape_vals, show_y = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = Term, y = Estimate, color = Tier, shape = Tier)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, color = "black", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_pointrange(aes(ymin = Estimate - 1.96 * `Std. Error`,
                        ymax = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error`),
                    position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 0.3) +
    geom_text(aes(label = stars, y = Estimate + 1.96 * `Std. Error` + 0.015),
              position = position_dodge(width = 0.6), size = 2.6, vjust = 0,
              color = "black", show.legend = FALSE) +
    scale_color_manual(values = color_vals) +
    scale_shape_manual(values = shape_vals) +
    coord_cartesian(ylim = c(-0.5, 0.5)) +
    labs(title = title_label, x = NULL,
         y = "Effect of Blinding (Δ Overall Z)",
         color = "Tier:", shape = "Tier:") +
    theme_bw(base_size = 8) +
    theme(axis.text.x  = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1, size = 7),
          axis.title.y = element_text(size = 8),
          legend.position = "top",
          legend.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 8),
          legend.text  = element_text(size = 7),
          plot.title   = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
          plot.margin  = margin(3, 6, 3, 3, "pt"))
  if (!show_y) p <- p + theme(axis.title.y = element_blank())
  p
}

gpa_levels <- c("Low_GPA", "Mid_GPA", "Top_GPA")
gpa_labels <- c("Low GPA", "Mid GPA", "Top GPA")
gpa_colors <- c("Low GPA" = "black", "Mid GPA" = "steelblue", "Top GPA" = "indianred1")
gpa_shapes <- c("Low GPA" = 16, "Mid GPA" = 15, "Top GPA" = 17)

code_levels <- c(0, 1)
code_labels <- c("Bottom 50% Coders", "Top 50% Coders")
code_colors <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = "black", "Top 50% Coders" = "indianred1")
code_shapes <- c("Bottom 50% Coders" = 16, "Top 50% Coders" = 17)

## Wrapper: fit the §2 demographic-effect regression on a sample with split.
fit_split <- function(d, factor_var, split_var) {
  suppressMessages(suppressWarnings(feols(
    as.formula(sprintf(
      "I(-sc_overall_z) ~ i(%s, ref = 'Blind') + resp_age + I(resp_age^2) + I(resp_age^3) | resume_index + r_do + resp_gender + resp_race + s2_educ + s2_income",
      factor_var)),
    data = d, split = as.formula(paste0("~", split_var)), vcov = ~ responseid)))
}

build_role_panel <- function(d, role_label, show_y_left = TRUE) {
  ## six fits per role
  m_rg_gpa  <- fit_split(d, "race_gender",     "gpa_tier3")
  m_rg_cod  <- fit_split(d, "race_gender",     "top_half_coder")
  m_ut_gpa  <- fit_split(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "gpa_tier3")
  m_ut_cod  <- fit_split(d, "urm_tech_blind",  "top_half_coder")
  m_ax_gpa  <- fit_split(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "gpa_tier3")
  m_ax_cod  <- fit_split(d, "urm_axis_blind",  "top_half_coder")

  df_rg_gpa <- extract_split_te(m_rg_gpa, "race_gender",    gpa_labels)
  df_rg_cod <- extract_split_te(m_rg_cod, "race_gender",    code_labels)
  df_ut_gpa <- extract_split_te(m_ut_gpa, "urm_tech_blind", gpa_labels)
  df_ut_cod <- extract_split_te(m_ut_cod, "urm_tech_blind", code_labels)
  df_ax_gpa <- extract_split_te(m_ax_gpa, "urm_axis_blind", gpa_labels)
  df_ax_cod <- extract_split_te(m_ax_cod, "urm_axis_blind", code_labels)

  p1 <- make_split_plot(df_rg_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": Per-Cell, by GPA"),
                        gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left)
  p2 <- make_split_plot(df_rg_cod, paste0(role_label, ": Per-Cell, by Coding"),
                        code_colors, code_shapes, show_y = FALSE)
  p3 <- make_split_plot(df_ut_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": URM-tech, by GPA"),
                        gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left)
  p4 <- make_split_plot(df_ut_cod, paste0(role_label, ": URM-tech, by Coding"),
                        code_colors, code_shapes, show_y = FALSE)
  p5 <- make_split_plot(df_ax_gpa, paste0(role_label, ": URM-axis, by GPA"),
                        gpa_colors, gpa_shapes, show_y = show_y_left)
  p6 <- make_split_plot(df_ax_cod, paste0(role_label, ": URM-axis, by Coding"),
                        code_colors, code_shapes, show_y = FALSE)

  list(rg_gpa = p1, rg_cod = p2,
       ut_gpa = p3, ut_cod = p4,
       ax_gpa = p5, ax_cod = p6)
}

panels_hr  <- build_role_panel(dt_hr,  "HR",  show_y_left = TRUE)
panels_eng <- build_role_panel(dt_eng, "Eng", show_y_left = TRUE)

## Save HR figure (3 rows × 2 cols).
fig_dir <- here("2_Reports", "figures")
if (!dir.exists(fig_dir)) dir.create(fig_dir, recursive = TRUE)
plot_file_hr  <- file.path(fig_dir, "8B_het_by_quality_HR.pdf")
plot_file_eng <- file.path(fig_dir, "8B_het_by_quality_Eng.pdf")

fig_hr <- (panels_hr$rg_gpa + panels_hr$rg_cod) /
          (panels_hr$ut_gpa + panels_hr$ut_cod) /
          (panels_hr$ax_gpa + panels_hr$ax_cod) +
          plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
          theme(legend.position = "top")

fig_eng <- (panels_eng$rg_gpa + panels_eng$rg_cod) /
           (panels_eng$ut_gpa + panels_eng$ut_cod) /
           (panels_eng$ax_gpa + panels_eng$ax_cod) +
           plot_layout(guides = "collect") &
           theme(legend.position = "top")

suppressMessages(suppressWarnings({
  ggsave(plot_file_hr,  plot = fig_hr,  width = 10, height = 9, device = "pdf")
  ggsave(plot_file_eng, plot = fig_eng, width = 10, height = 9, device = "pdf")
}))

cat("\\noindent\\textbf{HR Evaluators}\\par\\vspace{0.2em}\n")
cat("\\begin{center}\\includegraphics[width=0.85\\textwidth]{", plot_file_hr, "}\\end{center}\n", sep = "")
cat("\\clearpage\n")
cat("\\noindent\\textbf{Engineer Evaluators}\\par\\vspace{0.2em}\n")
cat("\\begin{center}\\includegraphics[width=0.85\\textwidth]{", plot_file_eng, "}\\end{center}\n", sep = "")

rm(dt_hr, dt_eng, build_aux, extract_split_te, make_split_plot, fit_split, build_role_panel,
   gpa_levels, gpa_labels, gpa_colors, gpa_shapes,
   code_levels, code_labels, code_colors, code_shapes,
   panels_hr, panels_eng, fig_hr, fig_eng,
   plot_file_hr, plot_file_eng, fig_dir)
```
